expectations.text: Attacks the strongest claims first (decision compression, demo-as-spec), not peripheral weaknesses
expectations.evidence: Lead challenge targets 'decision compression' — argues it's 'decision front-loading' not compression. Strong opening.

expectations.text: Provides the strongest version of the opposing view — not a straw man but a real argument someone would make
expectations.evidence: Counter-arguments are fully developed. E.g., challenge 3 argues the behavioral change matters more than the tool.

expectations.text: Challenges survivorship bias — only two projects, both presented as successes
expectations.evidence: Challenge 2 directly addresses sample size of two with both successes.

expectations.text: Questions whether outcomes should be attributed to Claude Code or to the author's effort/context
expectations.evidence: Challenge 3 argues stripping Claude Code shows the practice, not the tool, is the real lesson.

expectations.text: Classifies each challenge as fatal flaw, weakness to acknowledge, or valid tradeoff
expectations.evidence: All 6 challenges have explicit verdicts using the exact terminology.

expectations.text: Ends with 'The lesson survives if...' conditions
expectations.evidence: 'The lesson survives if:' section with 5 conditions.
